_description: Design tokens for the text-on-gradient pattern. All values are used in after/styles.css. Contrast ratios noted per token.

start.usage: Gradient dark end — hero banner background start color
start.note: Near-black navy. White text at this end of the gradient = 19.5:1.

end.usage: Gradient light end — hero banner background end color
end.note: Deep navy blue. White text at this end of the gradient = 10.4:1. Always test the LIGHTEST gradient endpoint for white text.

fail-example-start.usage: Was the dark end in before/ — acceptable at this end

fail-example-end.usage: Was the light end in before/ — white text fails completely at this point
fail-example-end.note: Sky blue 300. Looks like a 'blue' but is actually nearly as light as white. Never use as a surface for white text.

on-dark-gradient.usage: All text on the dark gradient hero
on-dark-gradient.note: White on #0f172a = 19.5:1 (AAA). White on #1e3a8a = 10.4:1 (AAA). Safe throughout.

primary-background.usage: Primary CTA button background on dark hero
primary-background.note: Flipped: light button on dark background. Provides strong visual contrast against the hero.

primary-text.usage: Dark text on white primary button

ghost-border.usage: Ghost button border + text on dark hero background
ghost-border.note: White border on #0f172a = 19.5:1. White border on #1e3a8a = 10.4:1. Both pass WCAG 1.4.11 (3:1 required).

fail-example-primary.usage: Shown in before/ — light end of gradient used as button bg with white text. Fails.

fail-example-ghost.usage: Shown in before/ — another light gradient color as button bg with white text. Fails.
